Situation: 1. They take hours or days to text back.
Good Advice: Match their energy and communicate your needs. If they remain inconsistent, move on.
Bad Advice: Double text, spam their phone, or demand immediate replies.

Situation: 2. Partner doesn't want to define the relationship.
Good Advice: Have a direct conversation about your boundaries; don't settle for less than you want.
Bad Advice: Give an aggressive ultimatum or wait endlessly hoping they change.

Situation: 3. I'm terrified of rejection so I haven't asked my crush out.
Good Advice: Shoot your shot casually. Respect a 'no', give it space, and the friendship will survive.
Bad Advice: Confess your undying love in a massive, public grand gesture.

Situation: 4. We fight constantly over small things.
Good Advice: Use 'I' statements and seek to understand the root cause. Consider couples counseling.
Bad Advice: Give them the silent treatment until they figure out what they did wrong.

Situation: 5. Caught them lying about their whereabouts.
Good Advice: Confront them calmly with the facts. If they gaslight you or deny reality, leave.
Bad Advice: Snoop through their phone to find more evidence before speaking up.

Situation: 6. Partner wants to spend every waking second together.
Good Advice: Communicate your need for independence. A healthy relationship requires space.
Bad Advice: Dump your friends and hobbies to prove your loyalty.

Situation: 7. My date talks about their ex constantly.
Good Advice: Note it as a red flag that they aren't over them. Politely change the subject or end the date.
Bad Advice: Try to prove you are smarter or better looking than their ex.

Situation: 8. I always pay for everything on our dates.
Good Advice: Have an honest, non-accusatory talk about financial expectations and splitting costs.
Bad Advice: Resent them silently while continuing to pay for everything.

Situation: 9. They ghosted me after three great dates.
Good Advice: Accept that their silence is an answer. Maintain your dignity and move on.
Bad Advice: Stalk their social media and send paragraphs begging for closure.

Situation: 10. We have totally different political views.
Good Advice: Decide if this is a core-value dealbreaker. If not, agree to respectful disagreements.
Bad Advice: Try to aggressively debate them into changing their worldview.

Situation: 11. My partner is still friends with their ex.
Good Advice: Assess if the friendship has healthy boundaries. Communicate any insecurities you feel.
Bad Advice: Demand they block the ex immediately to prove their love.

Situation: 12. They are perfect but bad in bed.
Good Advice: Communicate your physical needs kindly and guide them on what you like.
Bad Advice: Fake it forever or cheat on them.

Situation: 13. I found out they are still active on dating apps.
Good Advice: Ask them about it directly if you already agreed on exclusivity.
Bad Advice: Make a fake profile to catch them or catfish them.

Situation: 14. Their family doesn't like me.
Good Advice: Be polite and give it time, but ensure your partner sets boundaries and defends you.
Bad Advice: Start drama with their parents or force your partner to choose.

Situation: 15. We have mismatched sex drives.
Good Advice: Find a middle ground through open, non-judgmental discussion, or see a sex therapist.
Bad Advice: Guilt-trip or pressure them into intimacy.

Situation: 16. My partner hates my best friend.
Good Advice: Keep the relationships separate but demand basic respect from both sides.
Bad Advice: Force them to hang out and be besties.

Situation: 17. They want kids and I don't.
Good Advice: Recognize this as a fundamental incompatibility and part ways amicably.
Bad Advice: Stay and hope they eventually change their mind.

Situation: 18. I feel like I'm putting in all the effort.
Good Advice: Stop over-functioning. Express how the imbalance makes you feel exhausted.
Bad Advice: Keep over-giving to win their affection while building resentment.

Situation: 19. My partner is extremely jealous of my coworkers.
Good Advice: Reassure them, but maintain your professional boundaries and friendships.
Bad Advice: Quit your job or isolate yourself to soothe their anxiety.

Situation: 20. They criticized my body or appearance.
Good Advice: Set a hard boundary that body-shaming is entirely unacceptable.
Bad Advice: Go on an extreme diet or change your look to please them.

Situation: 21. We never go on romantic dates anymore.
Good Advice: Take the initiative to plan a date night and express your need to keep the romance alive.
Bad Advice: Complain constantly that they aren't romantic enough.

Situation: 22. They keep liking suggestive photos online.
Good Advice: Explain how it makes you feel disrespected without being accusatory.
Bad Advice: Hack their account or post revenge traps online.

Situation: 23. My partner earns significantly less money than me.
Good Advice: Treat them as an equal and find a lifestyle and date-budget that works for both of you.
Bad Advice: Use your money to control relationship decisions.

Situation: 24. They hide their phone screen when I walk by.
Good Advice: Ask them directly why they are being secretive and express your concern.
Bad Advice: Wait until they fall asleep and unlock their phone.

Situation: 25. I caught feelings for a friend with benefits.
Good Advice: Be honest about your changing feelings. Be ready to walk away if they don't match.
Bad Advice: Hide your feelings and pretend you're fine with casual sex.

Situation: 26. My partner struggles with severe anxiety/depression.
Good Advice: Offer support and encourage professional help, but remember you aren't their therapist.
Bad Advice: Try to 'fix' them yourself or take their low moods personally.

Situation: 27. They want to move in, but I'm not ready.
Good Advice: Be honest about your timeline. Don't rush into cohabitation if you aren't comfortable.
Bad Advice: Move in anyway just to avoid disappointing them.

Situation: 28. My partner is terrible with money and in debt.
Good Advice: Discuss financial habits openly before ever merging finances or moving in.
Bad Advice: Pay off their debt secretly or give them unrestricted access to your cards.

Situation: 29. They have a child from a previous relationship.
Good Advice: Accept that the child comes first. Take introductions slowly and let the child set the pace.
Bad Advice: Compete with the child for your partner's attention.

Situation: 30. I feel bored in my long-term relationship.
Good Advice: Introduce new shared hobbies and adventures to reignite the spark.
Bad Advice: Start an emotional affair with a coworker for excitement.

Situation: 31. My partner constantly interrupts me.
Good Advice: Point it out gently in the moment ('I wasn't finished speaking, please let me finish').
Bad Advice: Yell at them or completely stop talking and shut down.

Situation: 32. They won't post me on social media.
Good Advice: Discuss what social media means to you both. Ascertain if they are hiding you or just private.
Bad Advice: Post a million photos of them and tag them aggressively to mark your territory.

Situation: 33. We disagree on how to split household chores.
Good Advice: Create a clear, mutually agreed-upon chore chart or system that feels fair.
Bad Advice: Weaponize incompetence or do it all yourself while seething.

Situation: 34. My partner flirts with other people in front of me.
Good Advice: Address the disrespect immediately in private and set a firm boundary.
Bad Advice: Flirt with someone else right in front of them to make them jealous.

Situation: 35. They are pressuring me to have sex before I'm ready.
Good Advice: Stand your ground. A caring partner will respect your pace unconditionally.
Bad Advice: Give in to the pressure just to avoid losing them.

Situation: 36. I think I'm just a rebound.
Good Advice: Take things very slowly and look for signs they are truly over their ex.
Bad Advice: Push for a serious commitment immediately to lock them down.

Situation: 37. My partner snooped through my phone.
Good Advice: Have a serious talk about trust, insecurity, and the invasion of privacy.
Bad Advice: Change all your passwords and immediately snoop through theirs in retaliation.

Situation: 38. They never apologize when they are wrong.
Good Advice: Explain that accountability is necessary for the relationship to survive.
Bad Advice: Let it go every single time just to keep the peace.

Situation: 39. We are long distance and communication is fading.
Good Advice: Schedule regular virtual dates and check-ins to stay intentionally connected.
Bad Advice: Assume they are cheating and act passive-aggressively when they do call.

Situation: 40. My partner is a workaholic.
Good Advice: Discuss carving out dedicated, uninterrupted time for the relationship.
Bad Advice: Show up at their office unannounced to demand their attention.

Situation: 41. They joke about my insecurities.
Good Advice: Tell them clearly that the 'jokes' hurt and must stop immediately.
Bad Advice: Laugh along while it quietly destroys your self-esteem.

Situation: 42. I don't find my partner physically attractive anymore.
Good Advice: Focus on building emotional intimacy and see if the physical spark returns naturally.
Bad Advice: Tell them harshly that they let themselves go.

Situation: 43. My partner makes all the decisions.
Good Advice: Start asserting your preferences on small things (dinner, movies) and build up from there.
Bad Advice: Let them control everything because it's easier to avoid conflict.

Situation: 44. They bring up my past mistakes during new arguments.
Good Advice: Ask to stay on the current topic and leave resolved issues in the past.
Bad Advice: Bring up their past mistakes in retaliation to even the score.

Situation: 45. My friends said they saw my partner on a date.
Good Advice: Ask your partner directly about the incident and gauge their reaction.
Bad Advice: Confront the alleged 'date' in public without talking to your partner first.

Situation: 46. We have different religious beliefs.
Good Advice: Determine early on if you can build a life that respects both worldviews.
Bad Advice: Try to forcefully convert them to your religion.

Situation: 47. My partner gave me an ultimatum.
Good Advice: Evaluate if the request is a healthy boundary or a toxic control tactic.
Bad Advice: Cave immediately out of fear of being alone.

Situation: 48. I want to break up but I don't want to hurt them.
Good Advice: Be honest, direct, and compassionate. Rip the band-aid off clearly.
Bad Advice: Ghost them or act terribly so they are forced to break up with you.

Situation: 49. They are overly critical of everything I do.
Good Advice: Call out the critical behavior and explain how it erodes your emotional safety.
Bad Advice: Agree with them and apologize constantly for not being perfect.

Situation: 50. My partner has an untreated substance abuse issue.
Good Advice: Set firm boundaries and encourage professional help. Protect your own peace first.
Bad Advice: Enable them, make excuses for them, and hide their addiction from others.
